overview: The Village of Weston Plan Commission approved the site plan for the new Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive, and adopted a new salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection areas. Both items passed unanimously with the commission addressing technical details about gas line easements and future building expansion possibilities.

agenda.item: Call to order and roll call
Site plan review - Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive
Creation of salt storage shed inspection program - Wellhead protection district
April 2026 staff approved certified survey map and site plan
Planning and development department monthly project update report

discussions.item: Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility site plan
discussions.body: Staff presented the site plan for the new police department at 3600 Community Center Drive, noting it meets all B2 zoning requirements. Chief Jeremy explained the building was rotated to face east due to a gas pipeline underneath the parking lot, which significantly reduced fill costs. The building will accommodate 50-60 officers for future growth beyond the current 40 officers. Discussion included an alternate storage shed location and future expansion possibilities to the north. Motion to approve was made and passed unanimously with conditions 2 through 5 as listed by staff.

discussions.item: Salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
discussions.body: Jennifer presented a new inspection program requiring two annual inspections per facility - one warm weather inspection focused on structural integrity and one winter operational inspection focused on material handling practices. The program includes a standardized inspection worksheet similar to DOT standards. Staff noted one existing facility near Sternberg has never gone through the wellhead protection conditional use process and agreed to reach out to them. Motion to approve passed unanimously with an amendment to report back to both the Plan Commission and Public Works Committee.
Motion to approve the April 13, 2026 meeting minutes was made and seconded. Passed unanimously without discussion.

discussions.item: April 2026 staff approved items and building permits
discussions.body: Both the certified survey map/site plan report and building permit report for April 2026 were approved unanimously without discussion.

Jennifer announced the first meeting of the joint Plan Commission/Village Board steering committee for the comprehensive plan is scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 5:00 p.m. Commissioner discussion followed regarding potential residential TIF districts for workforce housing, with staff noting Ehlers has prepared a presentation on qualifications that could be shared with the commission.

publicComment: Jim Pensson of 5002 Arrow Street, Weston, spoke via video call requesting the commission use common sense regarding landscaping requirements for the police department project to avoid excessive taxpayer costs similar to the Green Heck Turner building. He also requested annual reporting on the salt shed inspection program, noting some zoning complaints are years old and get 'lost in the sauce.'
actionItems: Site plan for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive approved with staff conditions
Salt storage shed inspection program adopted for wellhead protection district with two annual inspections per facility
Staff to report inspection program findings back to Plan Commission and Public Works Committee in approximately one year
Staff to reach out to existing salt storage facility near Sternberg that has not gone through wellhead protection conditional use process
Joint Plan Commission/Village Board steering committee meeting scheduled for June 1st at 5:00 p.m.
Staff to send email and calendar event for steering committee meeting
Electric vehicle charging stations added as future agenda item for fall
Next regular meeting scheduled for Monday, June 8th, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.
title: Plan Commission
